displayTitle: Contrast: The Anatomy of a Good Radiograph

beats.narration: Every radiograph faces one question: can you see the smallest detail that matters? That ability is radiographic sensitivity.

payload.title: SENSITIVITY
payload.sub: The bottom line of image quality.

beats.narration: Sensitivity stands on two pillars: contrast and definition. Today: contrast. And contrast itself combines two contributions: subject contrast and film contrast.
Three things set subject contrast in the part and beam: thickness differences, radiation energy, where lower means more contrast, and scatter fogging it away.
Film contrast is the slope of the characteristic curve, and the slope climbs as density rises. That's the real reason codes set a minimum density.
Two layers: energy and scatter set subject contrast. Film, density, and processing set film contrast. Get both right and sensitivity takes care of itself.

payload.sub: Get both right — sensitivity takes care of itself.
